title: impatiens mackeyana subsp. mackeyana
intro: Impatiens mackeyana subsp. mackeyana, also known as Balsam, is a perennial flowering plant native to the tropical regions of the world. The plant is known for its medicinal properties, particularly its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which are used to treat various health issues such as skin irritations, wounds, and respiratory problems. The health benefits of Impatiens mackeyana subsp. mackeyana include reducing fever, alleviating pain, and promoting wound healing, making it a valuable herb in traditional medicine. Herbal preparations of the plant include infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which can be used to treat various health conditions.
